docs.text:
• RoutePlannerResultEditor
  • Route Editor Violations
  • AgentPlan.getViolations()

docs.title: Example: Editing Planned Result After Optimization
docs.text:
In this example, we'll:

  • Execute a route optimization
  • Inspect the result
  • Reassign a job from one agent to another using the RoutePlannerResultEditor
  • Use different strategies for modifications

This is useful when you want to manually adjust plans based on real-world constraints (like agent availability or new priorities).

docs.title: 1. Import the SDK
docs.text: import { RoutePlanner, Agent, Job, RoutePlannerResultEditor } from "@geoapify/route-planner-sdk";

docs.title: 2. Initialize Planner and Add Input
docs.text:
const planner = new RoutePlanner({ apiKey: "YOUR_API_KEY" });

planner
  .setMode("drive")
  .addAgent(new Agent().setId("agent-1").setStartLocation(13.40, 52.52))
  .addAgent(new Agent().setId("agent-2").setStartLocation(13.42, 52.50))
  .addJob(new Job().setId("job-1").setLocation(13.41, 52.51))
  .addJob(new Job().setId("job-2").setLocation(13.39, 52.53));

docs.title: 3. Plan the Route
docs.text: const result = await planner.plan();

docs.title: 4. View Initial Assignments
docs.text:
result.getAgentPlans().forEach(agent => {
  if (!agent) return;
  console.log(`Agent: ${agent.getAgentId()}`);
  agent.getActions().forEach(action => {
    const jobId = action.getJobId();
    if (!jobId) return;
    console.log(` - ${action.getType()} ${jobId}`);
  });
});

docs.title: 5. Reassign a Job to Another Agent
docs.text:
Let's say we want to reassign job-1 from agent-1 to agent-2:

const editor = new RoutePlannerResultEditor(result);

// Default: full reoptimization (Route Planner API)
await editor.assignJobs("agent-2", ["job-1"]);

const updatedResult = editor.getModifiedResult();

docs.title: 6. Using Different Strategies
docs.text: The editor supports different strategies depending on your needs:

docs.title: Full Reoptimization (Route Planner API)
docs.text:
Use when you want the best possible route after changes:

const editor = new RoutePlannerResultEditor(result);

// Full reoptimization with Route Planner API
await editor.assignJobs("agent-2", ["job-1"], { strategy: 'reoptimize' });

docs.title: PreserveOrder Strategy
docs.text: The preserveOrder strategy is flexible and provides three different behaviors:

docs.title: 1. Find Optimal Insertion Point (Route Matrix API)
docs.text:
Use when you want a good position without reordering existing stops:

const editor = new RoutePlannerResultEditor(result);

// Find optimal insertion point using Route Matrix API
await editor.assignJobs("agent-2", ["job-1"], { strategy: 'preserveOrder' });

docs.title: 2. Insert Near Specific Position (Route Matrix API)
docs.text:
Use when you want insertion constrained to a route segment after a known stop:

const editor = new RoutePlannerResultEditor(result);

// Optimize insertion after a specific job (Route Matrix API)
await editor.assignJobs("agent-2", ["job-1"], { 
  strategy: 'preserveOrder', 
  afterId: 'job-2' 
});

// Or insert after a specific waypoint
await editor.assignJobs("agent-2", ["job-1"], { 
  strategy: 'preserveOrder', 
  afterWaypointIndex: 0  // After start waypoint (first position)
});

docs.title: 3. Append to End (No API Call, Fastest)
docs.text:
Use for quick UI updates or when order doesn't matter:

const editor = new RoutePlannerResultEditor(result);

// Append to end of route (no API call)
await editor.assignJobs("agent-2", ["job-1"], { 
  strategy: 'preserveOrder', 
  append: true 
});

docs.title: PreserveOrder for Removal
docs.text:
Use when removing jobs/shipments without reordering remaining stops:

const editor = new RoutePlannerResultEditor(result);

// Remove without reordering (no API call)
await editor.removeJobs(["job-1"], { strategy: 'preserveOrder' });

docs.title: 7. View Modified Assignments
docs.text:
console.log("Modified solution:");

updatedResult.getAgentPlans().forEach(agent => {
  if (!agent) return;
  console.log(`Agent: ${agent.getAgentId()}`);
  agent.getActions().forEach(action => {
    const jobId = action.getJobId();
    if (!jobId) return;
    console.log(` - ${action.getType()} ${jobId}`);
  });
});

docs.title: Strategy Comparison
docs.text: Strategy Speed API Calls Best For reoptimize Slowest Route Planner API Finding optimal route preserveOrder (no position) Medium Route Matrix API Quick optimal insertion without reordering preserveOrder (afterId/afterWaypointIndex) Medium Route Matrix API Insert after a known point with local order preserved preserveOrder (append: true) Fastest None Direct insert after position or append to route end

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
This example shows how you can:

  • Automatically optimize a plan
  • Manually override assignments using editor tools
  • Choose the right strategy based on your needs:
  • reoptimize for best results (slower)
  • preserveOrder for flexible, fast modifications

This gives you the flexibility to combine automated and human planning.

For more editor capabilities, see RoutePlannerResultEditor.

docs.title: Example: Planning 20 Shipments with 3 Agents
docs.text:
This example demonstrates how to use the Geoapify Route Optimization SDK to:

  • Create multiple agents
  • Define 20 shipments with predefined pickup and delivery points
  • Optimize delivery plans for 3 agents

docs.title: 1. Import the SDK
docs.text: import { RoutePlanner, Agent, Shipment, ShipmentStep } from "@geoapify/route-planner-sdk";

docs.title: 2. Create the Planner
docs.text: const planner = new RoutePlanner({ apiKey: "YOUR_API_KEY" });

docs.title: 3. Add Agents
docs.text:
Each agent has a different starting point:

planner
  .addAgent(new Agent().setId("agent-1").setStartLocation(13.40, 52.52))
  .addAgent(new Agent().setId("agent-2").setStartLocation(13.42, 52.50))
  .addAgent(new Agent().setId("agent-3").setStartLocation(13.39, 52.53));

docs.title: 4. Add 20 Shipments with Predefined Coordinates
docs.text:
Below are example coordinates for pickups and deliveries. You can replace them with real-world locations.

const shipmentCoords = [
  { pickup: [13.35, 52.50], delivery: [13.45, 52.55] },
  { pickup: [13.36, 52.51], delivery: [13.44, 52.54] },
  { pickup: [13.37, 52.52], delivery: [13.43, 52.53] },
  { pickup: [13.38, 52.53], delivery: [13.42, 52.52] },
  { pickup: [13.39, 52.54], delivery: [13.41, 52.51] },
  { pickup: [13.40, 52.55], delivery: [13.40, 52.50] },
  { pickup: [13.41, 52.50], delivery: [13.39, 52.55] },
  { pickup: [13.42, 52.51], delivery: [13.38, 52.54] },
  { pickup: [13.43, 52.52], delivery: [13.37, 52.53] },
  { pickup: [13.44, 52.53], delivery: [13.36, 52.52] },
  { pickup: [13.45, 52.54], delivery: [13.35, 52.51] },
  { pickup: [13.34, 52.55], delivery: [13.33, 52.50] },
  { pickup: [13.32, 52.52], delivery: [13.31, 52.53] },
  { pickup: [13.30, 52.51], delivery: [13.29, 52.54] },
  { pickup: [13.28, 52.50], delivery: [13.27, 52.55] },
  { pickup: [13.26, 52.53], delivery: [13.25, 52.52] },
  { pickup: [13.24, 52.54], delivery: [13.23, 52.51] },
  { pickup: [13.22, 52.55], delivery: [13.21, 52.50] },
  { pickup: [13.20, 52.52], delivery: [13.19, 52.53] },
  { pickup: [13.18, 52.51], delivery: [13.17, 52.54] },
];

shipmentCoords.forEach(({ pickup, delivery }, i) => {
  const shipment = new Shipment()
    .setId(`shipment-${i + 1}`)
    .setPickup(new ShipmentStep().setLocation(pickup[0], pickup[1]).setDuration(60))
    .setDelivery(new ShipmentStep().setLocation(delivery[0], delivery[1]).setDuration(60));

  planner.addShipment(shipment);
});

docs.title: 5. Plan and Analyze
docs.text:
const result = await planner.setMode("drive").plan();

result.getAgentPlans().forEach((agent) => {
  if (!agent) return;
  console.log(`Agent: ${agent.getAgentId()}`);
  agent.getActions().forEach((action) => {
    const shipmentId = action.getShipmentId();
    if (!shipmentId) return;
    console.log(` - ${action.getType()} shipment ${shipmentId} at ${action.getStartTime()}s`);
  });
});

console.log("Unassigned shipments:", result.getUnassignedShipments().length);

docs.title: Result
docs.text:
You now have an optimized plan that distributes 20 shipments across 3 agents, balancing their travel time and minimizing route overlap.

To visualize the output, see RoutePlannerTimeline.

For editing the result manually, use RoutePlannerResultEditor.

docs.title: Example: Optimizing Delivery Routes with Two Agents
docs.text:
This example demonstrates how to use the Geoapify Route Optimization SDK to:

  • Define multiple delivery jobs
  • Assign two delivery agents
  • Send a request to the Route Planner API
  • Print the optimized job sequence for each agent

docs.title: 1. Import the SDK
docs.text:
Import it in your TypeScript project:

import RoutePlanner, { Agent, Job } from "@geoapify/route-planner-sdk";

docs.title: 2. Create Your Planner Instance
docs.text:
You need to pass your API key:

const planner = new RoutePlanner({ apiKey: "YOUR_API_KEY" });

docs.title: 3. Define Agents
docs.text:
Each agent has a unique id and a starting location (longitude, latitude). You can also define working hours or vehicle capacity.

planner
  .addAgent(new Agent()
    .setId("agent-1")
    .setStartLocation(13.4050, 52.5200))  // Berlin
  .addAgent(new Agent()
    .setId("agent-2")
    .setStartLocation(13.3889, 52.5170)); // Berlin Mitte

docs.title: 4. Add Jobs
docs.text:
Each job is a delivery task. Specify location and optional details like duration or priority.

planner
  .addJob(new Job().setId("job-1").setLocation(13.4105, 52.5190))
  .addJob(new Job().setId("job-2").setLocation(13.3969, 52.5145))
  .addJob(new Job().setId("job-3").setLocation(13.4285, 52.5232));

docs.title: 5. Plan the Route
docs.text:
Call .plan() to send the request and receive an optimized route plan.

const result = await planner.setMode("drive").plan();

docs.title: 6. Inspect the Result
docs.text:
Print a simplified summary of the plan:

for (const agent of result.getAgentPlans()) {
  if (!agent) continue;
  console.log(`Agent: ${agent.getAgentId()}`);
  for (const action of agent.getActions()) {
    console.log(` - ${action.getType()} at time ${action.getStartTime()}`);
  }
}

This will show when each agent starts, what actions they perform, and in what order.

docs.title: What's Next?
docs.text:
You can also:

  • Visualize results with RoutePlannerTimeline
  • Modify assignments using RoutePlannerResultEditor
  • Rebalance workload dynamically

For more details, visit the API Overview.
